char.doc:
Wrapper library for functions operating on single-byte character values.

These functions are byte-oriented wrappers around the host C library. They
are not Unicode-aware character classification or case-mapping operations.

symbols.name: isdigit
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isdigit.

symbols.name: isalpha
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isalpha.

symbols.name: isalnum
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isalnum.

symbols.name: isspace
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isspace.

symbols.name: isupper
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isupper.

symbols.name: islower
symbols.doc: Wrapper around islower.

symbols.name: isxdigit
symbols.doc: Wrapper around isxdigit.

symbols.name: toupper
symbols.doc: Wrapper around toupper.

symbols.name: tolower
symbols.doc: Wrapper around tolower.

fs.doc:
Filesystem utilities.

This module provides filesystem object and directory operations.

symbols.name: exists
symbols.doc: Return whether a filesystem path exists.

symbols.name: is_file
symbols.doc: Return whether a filesystem path refers to a regular file.

symbols.name: is_dir
symbols.doc: Return whether a filesystem path refers to a directory.

symbols.name: mkdir
symbols.doc: Create a directory.

symbols.name: mkdir_all
symbols.doc: Create a directory path and any missing parents.

symbols.name: remove_file
symbols.doc: Remove a file.

symbols.name: remove_dir
symbols.doc: Remove an empty directory.

symbols.name: remove_dir_all
symbols.doc: Remove a directory tree recursively.

symbols.name: rename
symbols.doc: Rename or move a filesystem object.

symbols.name: copy_file
symbols.doc: Copy a file.

symbols.name: read_dir
symbols.doc:
Return the names of entries directly inside a directory.

Entry names are returned without directory prefixes.

symbols.name: temp_dir
symbols.doc: Return the platform temporary directory path.

io.doc:
File I/O utilities.

This module provides a minimal wrapper around basic C stdio file operations.
Supported file modes are currently `"r"` for reading, `"w"` for writing, and `"a"` for appending.
All operations throw on error.

symbols.name: File
symbols.doc: Opaque file handle.

symbols.name: open
symbols.signature: open(path: String, mode: String, region: Region) -> File
symbols.doc:
Open a file.

Supported modes are `"r"` (read), `"w"` (write), and `"a"` (append).

symbols.name: close
symbols.doc: Close an open file.

symbols.name: read
symbols.doc:
Read the full contents of a file.

The file must have been opened in read mode.

symbols.name: write
symbols.doc:
Write a string to a file.

The file must have been opened in write mode.

symbols.name: read_file
symbols.doc: Read an entire file by path.

symbols.name: write_file
symbols.signature: write_file(path: String, text: String, region: Region) -> void
symbols.doc: Write a string to a file by path, truncating existing contents.

math.doc:
Minimal scalar math utilities.

This module is a thin wrapper over a small subset of C `<math.h>`.
Trigonometric functions use radians.

Notes:
- Floating-point behavior follows the platform C math library (NaN/Inf propagation).
- `is_nan` / `is_finite` return `int` (0 or 1) to avoid assuming a specific `bool` ABI.

symbols.name: PI
symbols.doc: π (pi).

symbols.name: E
symbols.doc: Euler's number e.

symbols.name: abs
symbols.doc: Compute the absolute value.

symbols.name: approx_eq
symbols.signature: approx_eq(a: double, b: double, eps: double) -> bool
symbols.doc:
Check whether two values are equal within a tolerance.

Returns true if `abs(a - b) <= eps`.

symbols.name: min
symbols.doc: Return the smaller of two values.

symbols.name: max
symbols.doc: Return the larger of two values.

symbols.name: sqrt
symbols.doc: Compute the square root.

symbols.name: sin
symbols.doc: Compute the sine of an angle in radians.

symbols.name: cos
symbols.doc: Compute the cosine of an angle in radians.

symbols.name: tan
symbols.doc: Compute the tangent of an angle in radians.

symbols.name: atan2
symbols.doc:
Compute the arctangent of `y/x` using the signs of `y` and `x`.

The result is in radians.

symbols.name: floor
symbols.doc: Round down to the next integer value.

symbols.name: ceil
symbols.doc: Round up to the next integer value.

symbols.name: round
symbols.doc:
Round to the nearest integer value.

Halfway cases follow the platform C math library.

symbols.name: pow
symbols.doc: Raise a value to a power.

symbols.name: log
symbols.doc: Compute the natural logarithm.

symbols.name: exp
symbols.doc: Compute the natural exponential.

symbols.name: fmod
symbols.doc: Compute the floating-point remainder (C `fmod`).

symbols.name: is_nan
symbols.doc: Test whether the value is NaN.

symbols.name: is_finite
symbols.doc: Test whether the value is finite.

symbols.name: is_inf
symbols.doc: Test whether the value is positive or negative infinity.

symbols.name: join
symbols.doc:
Join path segments using the platform separator.

Empty segments are ignored.

symbols.name: basename
symbols.doc: Return the last path component.

symbols.name: dirname
symbols.doc:
Return the parent directory.

Root-like inputs are kept stable.
A path with no directory component returns `"."`.

symbols.name: extname
symbols.doc: Return the extension of the last path component.

symbols.name: is_absolute
symbols.doc:
Return whether `path` is absolute.

Windows paths accept both `\` and `/` separators.
Drive-root forms such as `C:\foo` are treated as absolute.

symbols.name: Rng
symbols.doc: Opaque random number generator state.

symbols.name: new
symbols.doc: Create a new RNG with a deterministic seed.

symbols.name: new_time
symbols.doc: Create a new RNG seeded from the current time.

symbols.name: next_int
symbols.doc: Generate a non-negative random integer.

symbols.name: next_double
symbols.doc: Generate a random floating-point number.

symbols.name: range_int
symbols.signature: range_int(rng: Rng, lo: int, hi: int) -> int
symbols.doc: Generate a random integer in a half-open interval.

region.doc:
Region allocator diagnostics.

This module exposes small read-only helpers for inspecting region allocator
state at runtime.

Notes:
- Reported sizes are allocator-level byte counts, not exact live object size.
- `used_bytes` includes allocator padding and dead bytes left behind by
region growth.
- These functions are intended for debugging and instrumentation.

symbols.name: used_bytes
symbols.doc: Return the number of allocator-consumed bytes in the region.

symbols.name: total_bytes
symbols.doc: Return the total capacity of all blocks currently owned by the region.

symbols.name: block_count
symbols.doc: Return the number of allocation blocks currently owned by the region.

symbols.name: print_stats
symbols.doc: Print a short region allocator summary to standard output.

symbols.name: panic
symbols.doc: Terminate the program with an error message.

symbols.name: input
symbols.doc: Read a line of input from standard input.

strbuf.doc:
Growable string buffer.

`StrBuf` stores a NUL-terminated byte buffer in an region. Growth allocates a
new buffer in the same region and copies existing content.

Notes:
- This is a byte buffer; it does not enforce UTF-8 boundaries.
- All allocations are performed in the provided `Region`.

symbols.name: StrBuf
symbols.doc: Opaque string buffer allocated in a `Region`.

symbols.name: new
symbols.doc: Create a new buffer from initial content.

symbols.name: append
symbols.doc: Append a string to the end of the buffer.

symbols.name: append_char
symbols.doc: Append a single byte to the end of the buffer.

symbols.name: pop
symbols.doc: Pop a single byte from the end of the buffer.

symbols.name: len
symbols.doc: Get the length of the buffer in bytes.

symbols.name: clear
symbols.doc:
Clears the buffer.

Does not deallocate or free anything, just resets the buffer pointer.

symbols.name: print
symbols.doc: Prints the buffer.

symbols.name: to_string
symbols.doc: Converts the buffer contents to a String.

string.doc:
String utilities.

`String` stores UTF-8 bytes, but the operations in this module are byte-based.
Lengths, indices, and slices refer to byte offsets, not Unicode characters.

symbols.name: slice
symbols.signature: slice(s: String, from: int, to: int, region: Region) -> String
symbols.doc: Return a substring of `s` from index `start` (inclusive) to `end` (exclusive).

symbols.name: to_int
symbols.doc: Convert a string to an integer.

symbols.name: to_double
symbols.doc: Convert a string to a double.

symbols.name: concat
symbols.signature: concat(left: String, right: String, region: Region) -> String
symbols.doc: Concatenate two strings.

symbols.name: replace
symbols.signature: replace(s: String, needle: String, repl: String, region: Region) -> String
symbols.doc:
Replace all occurrences of `needle` in `s` with `repl`.

If `needle` is empty, the function returns a copy of `s`.

symbols.name: copy
symbols.doc: Copies a string to new destination region.

symbols.name: starts_with
symbols.doc: Return whether `s` starts with `prefix`.

symbols.name: ends_with
symbols.doc: Return whether `s` ends with `suffix`.

symbols.name: trim
symbols.doc:
Return a copy of `s` with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

Whitespace is classified using `char::isspace`.

symbols.name: find
symbols.doc:
Find the first occurrence of `needle` in `s`.

Returns the start byte index of the first match, or `-1` if no match exists.

symbols.name: split
symbols.signature: split(s: String, delim: String, r: Region) -> Vec[String]
symbols.doc: Split a string by a delimiter.

symbols.name: join
symbols.signature: join(parts: Vec[String], delim: String, r: Region) -> String
symbols.doc:
Join strings from `parts` with `delim` inserted between adjacent elements.

If `parts` is empty, the function returns an empty string.

symbols.name: exit
symbols.doc: Terminate the process with a status code.

symbols.name: system
symbols.doc: Execute a command using the host system shell.

symbols.name: hasenv_raw
symbols.doc: Check whether an environment variable exists.

symbols.name: getenv_raw
symbols.doc: Get a present environment variable value.

symbols.name: getenv
symbols.doc: Get an environment variable value.

symbols.name: cwd
symbols.doc: Return the current working directory.

symbols.name: sleep
symbols.doc: Sleep for a number of milliseconds (approximate).

symbols.name: platform
symbols.doc: Return a short platform identifier string.

symbols.name: TestSuite
symbols.doc: Test suite state.

symbols.name: suite_assert
symbols.signature: suite_assert(suite: TestSuite, cond: bool, s: Site) -> void
symbols.doc: Test suite assertion function.

symbols.name: suite_finish
symbols.doc: Print suite summary and exit non-zero if any test failed.
